_README: Per-task, per-object physics configuration for add_physics_to_usds.py.
Run the baker with --task <name> and it bakes every USD in --input-dir
according to the object entry whose key matches the file name's suffix
(e.g. key 'ball' matches basketball_in_hoop_ball.usd, 'ball_stop' matches
..._ball_stop.usd; the longest matching key wins). Each object entry is
merged over '_defaults', so you only list what differs.
Fields:
type     : rigid      - movable rigid body the gripper can grasp
kinematic  - static collider that never moves (tables, hoops)
visual     - copied through with NO physics (decoration skins)
deformable - FEM soft body (EXPERIMENTAL, untested)
density  : kg/m^3; body weight = density * collider volume (used if mass null)
mass     : explicit mass (kg); set to override density. null = use density
static_friction, dynamic_friction, restitution : contact material
collider : dynamic collider shape (convexHull / convexDecomposition /
boundingCube / boundingSphere / sdf). Kinematic always uses the
exact mesh. Use 'sdf' for an accurate CONCAVE dynamic collider that
keeps holes (e.g. a ring threading onto a rod); convex hulls fill
the hole and eject. 'sdf_resolution' (default 256) tunes its detail.
collision : false authors NO collider (default true) so the robot passes
straight through the object. Use it to clear a base/holder out
of the arm's path while keeping the graspable object collidable.
compliant_stiffness, compliant_damping : >0 gives soft/springy contact
combine modes (average/min/multiply/max).
youngs_modulus, poissons_ratio : deformable stiffness (only type=deformable)
uv       : generate texture coords on a mesh that lacks them so an image
texture can map. 'auto' detects the shape (flat->planar,
ball->spherical, else box); or force 'spherical'/'box'/'planar'/'none'.
disable_gravity : true pins a pre-positioned rigid body in place (no fall/eject
before it is grasped). Only applies to rigid (non-kinematic).
ccd      : true enables continuous collision detection so a fast-falling thin
body cannot tunnel through a thin collider (e.g. the table top) and
explode out. Needs scene CCD on (run_scene enables it). Rigid only.
null = PhysX default (uncapped). A small value (~0.5) stops a thin
the collision shape and can push a threaded ring off its rod.
